title: 11 Best Herbal Teas For Stye
intro: Herbal teas for Stye are a natural remedy that involves drinking teas made from various herbs to treat and alleviate the symptoms of a stye, which is a painful and usually harmless lump or swelling on the eyelid or face. These herbal teas can effectively treat a stye due to their antibacterial, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic properties, which help to reduce swelling, ease pain, and combat bacterial infections. Examples of herbal teas that can be used to treat a stye include Echinacea tea, which has anti-inflammatory properties to reduce swelling; Ginger tea, which has antibacterial properties to combat infection; Green tea, which has antiseptic properties to prevent infection; Peppermint tea, which has cooling properties to ease pain and inflammation; and Licorice root tea, which has anti-inflammatory properties to reduce swelling. Additionally, other herbal teas such as Calendula, Sage, and Thyme can also be used to treat a stye, as they possess properties that help to soothe and calm the affected area.

preparations.herb_desc: Echinacea purpurea teas have been traditionally used to treat stye, a bacterial infection of the eyelid, due to their anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents present in these teas, such as alkylamides and caffeic acid derivatives, help to reduce inflammation and combat bacterial growth, thus alleviating the symptoms of stye. By consuming Echinacea purpurea teas, individuals can benefit from the antiseptic and antiviral properties, which aid in healing the affected area and preventing further complications. The benefits of using Echinacea purpurea teas to treat stye include reduced pain and swelling, accelerated recovery, and a lower risk of recurring infections.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh or dried Echinacea purpurea flowers.
Use 1 tablespoon of flowers for every 1 cup of boiling water.
Pour boiling water over the flowers in a cup or teapot.
Steep for 5-10 minutes, then strain the tea.
Drink 2-3 cups of the tea a day for 1 week to help reduce stye symptoms.
preparations.preparation_study: According to "Molecular nutrition & food research", Echinacea purpurea teas for stye may be safe for consumption due to their low potential to generate cytochrome P450 drug-herb interactions and the lack of verifiable reports of adverse events, suggesting a low risk to consumers.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Echinacea purpurea teas can be used to treat the stye ailment, but it may cause mild side effects such as allergic reactions, stomach upset, or nausea in some individuals, especially when consumed in excess or for extended periods. To minimize potential side effects, it is recommended to start with small doses and monitor your body's response, and also to avoid using Echinacea purpurea teas if you have sensitive skin or allergies.
preparation_usage: Boil 1 cup of water to make Echinacea purpurea tea.
Drink 1 cup of tea 3 times a day for 5 days.
Use 1 teaspoon of dried Echinacea purpurea per 1 cup of water.
Repeat the process for 2 weeks to see noticeable results.
Consult a doctor if the stye does not heal within 2 weeks.

preparations.herb_desc: Calendula officinalis teas have been used for centuries to treat various skin conditions, including styes, due to their anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Calendula officinalis, such as triterpenoid saponins and flavonoids, help to reduce swelling and fight off bacterial infections that cause styes. By applying a warm compress soaked in Calendula officinalis tea to the affected area, the herbal preparation can help to bring the stye to a head and promote drainage, ultimately leading to its resolution. The benefits of using Calendula officinalis tea to treat styes include its non-invasive and non-addictive nature, making it a popular choice for those seeking a natural remedy for this common skin condition.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Calendula officinalis flowers or 2 tablespoons of dried flowers.
Combine the flowers with 1 cup of boiling water in a heat-resistant cup.
Let the mixture steep for 5-7 minutes, then strain it using a fine mesh sieve.
Add 1 tablespoon of honey to the tea, if desired, to make it sweeter.
Drink the tea 2-3 times a day to help reduce inflammation and promote healing for stye.
preparations.preparation_study: According to "BMC veterinary research", Calendula officinalis teas may be beneficial for stye treatment due to its antibacterial and antifungal effects, which can help in reducing the infection and promoting wound healing.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Calendula officinalis teas can be used to treat styes due to their anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, but possible side effects include skin irritation, allergic reactions, and increased sensitivity to sunlight. To use Calendula officinalis teas safely, take precautions by starting with a small amount, avoiding application on broken skin, and being cautious when using it on sensitive areas to minimize the risk of adverse reactions.
preparation_usage: Soak a clean cloth in 1 cup of warm Calendula officinalis tea.
Gently apply the tea-soaked cloth to the affected eye for 10 minutes.
Repeat this process 3 times a day for 3 days.
Use 1 teaspoon of Calendula officinalis tea powder per 1 cup of boiling water.
Drink 1 cup of Calendula officinalis tea 3 times a day for 3 days.

preparations.herb_desc: Eucalyptus globulus teas have been traditionally used to treat stye, a painful and contagious bacterial infection of the eyelid. The antimicrobial properties of Eucalyptus globulus teas help to reduce the bacterial load and fight the infection, thus alleviating the symptoms of stye. The bioactive constituents of Eucalyptus globulus teas, including eucalyptol and caffeic acid, possess anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties that help to soothe and calm the affected area. The benefits of using Eucalyptus globulus teas to treat stye include reduced inflammation, pain, and swelling, as well as a lower risk of recurrence and complications.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of boiling water and 1 teaspoon of dried Eucalyptus globulus leaves.
Steep the Eucalyptus leaves in the boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea mixture into a cup using a fine-mesh sieve or tea infuser.
Add 1 tablespoon of honey (optional) to the tea for taste and mix well.
Drink the tea 2-3 times a day to help reduce the swelling and pain of a sty.
preparations.preparation_study: N/A
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Eucalyptus globulus teas can be used to treat stye, but its use may lead to side effects such as allergic reactions, digestive issues, and increased heart rate. To use this herbal preparation safely, it is recommended to start with small amounts, avoid consuming it for extended periods, and be cautious of interactions with other medications, as well as to monitor for symptoms of sensitivity, such as skin rashes or respiratory problems.
preparation_usage: Soak a clean cloth in hot Eucalyptus globulus tea.
Apply the cloth to the stye for 5-10 minutes, 3 times a day.
Use 1 teaspoon of dried Eucalyptus globulus leaves per cup of boiling water.
Steep the tea for 5-7 minutes, then strain and cool.
Continue using the tea compress for 2-3 days to help clear the stye.

preparations.herb_desc: Melaleuca alternifolia teas, also known as tea tree oil infusions, have been traditionally used to treat stye, a bacterial infection of the eyelid. The antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties of this herbal preparation help to combat the infection and reduce swelling, making it an effective remedy for this ailment. The bioactive constituents, including cineole and terpinen-4-ol, exhibit potent antibacterial activity, inhibiting the growth of Staphylococcus aureus and other pathogens responsible for stye. By incorporating Melaleuca alternifolia teas into their treatment regimen, individuals can benefit from reduced pain, inflammation, and risk of complications associated with stye.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 teaspoon of dried Melaleuca alternifolia leaves and 1 cup of boiling water.
Steep the leaves in the boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the mixture using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth.
Add 1 tablespoon of honey (optional) to the tea and mix well.
Drink the tea 2-3 times a day for 3-5 days to help relieve a sty.
preparations.preparation_study: N/A
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Melaleuca alternifolia teas can be used to treat the stye ailment, but possible side effects include gastrointestinal upset, allergic reactions, and interactions with certain medications, particularly blood thinners, due to the presence of compounds like cineole and terpinen-4-ol. To minimize potential risks, it is recommended to use high-quality Melaleuca alternifolia teas, start with small amounts, and avoid consuming the tea on an empty stomach, as this may exacerbate gastrointestinal side effects.
preparation_usage: Soak a cotton ball in 1-2 tablespoons of Melaleuca alternifolia tea.
Apply the cotton ball directly to the stye for 5-10 minutes.
Repeat the process 2-3 times a day for 3-5 days.
Use warm compresses on the stye for 5-10 minutes before applying tea.
Consult a doctor if the stye persists or worsens after 5 days.

preparations.herb_desc: Lavandula angustifolia teas have been traditionally used to treat the stye ailment, which is characterized by a painful, pus-filled bump on the eyelid. The anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties of this herbal preparation help to reduce swelling, ease pain, and prevent infection of the stye. The bioactive constituents, including linalool and linalyl acetate, have been shown to inhibit the growth of bacteria and reduce inflammation, thereby promoting the healing of the stye. Drinking Lavandula angustifolia teas may also help to soothe the affected area and reduce the risk of scarring, making it a popular natural remedy for treating styes.
preparation_procedure: Get 1 cup of water and bring it to a boil.
Add 2 tablespoons of dried Lavandula angustifolia flowers to a tea infuser or a heat-resistant cup.
Pour the boiling water over the Lavandula flowers and let it steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup and discard the flowers.
Let the tea cool down to lukewarm temperature before applying it to the stye with a clean cotton ball.
preparations.preparation_study: N/A
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Lavandula angustifolia teas can be used to treat the stye ailment due to its anti-inflammatory properties, but it may cause allergic reactions in some individuals, such as skin irritation, itching, or hives, especially if used in high concentrations or over an extended period. To use Lavandula angustifolia teas safely, it is essential to start with small amounts and gradually increase the dose, avoid using it on broken skin, and wash hands thoroughly after handling the herbal preparation to minimize potential side effects.
preparation_usage: Soak a clean cloth in 1 cup of cooled Lavandula angustifolia tea.
Apply the cloth over the closed eye with a stye for 5-7 minutes.
Repeat step 2 2-3 times a day for 2-3 days.
Use 2-3 teaspoons of dried Lavandula angustifolia flowers to make 1 cup of tea.
Steep the tea in boiling water for 5-7 minutes, then let it cool.

preparations.herb_desc: Aloe barbadensis teas have been traditionally used to treat stye, a painful bacterial infection of the eyelid or tear duct, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The gel-like substance extracted from the aloe vera plant is rich in bioactive constituents such as aloin, aloe-emodin, and vitamin E, which help to reduce redness, swelling, and pain associated with stye. Aloe barbadensis teas also have antiseptic properties that inhibit the growth of bacteria, thereby promoting the healing process and preventing further infection. The benefits of using aloe barbadensis teas to treat stye include reduced inflammation, faster healing, and reduced risk of complications, making it a popular natural remedy for this common ailment.
preparation_procedure: Cut a 2-inch piece of Aloe barbadensis leaf and remove the yellow sap.
Grate the aloe leaf using a fine grater or food processor to get 1/4 cup of pulp.
Add the aloe pulp to 1 cup of boiling water and reduce the heat to low.
Steep the mixture for 10-15 minutes, then strain it into a cup using a fine-mesh sieve.
Drink the tea 2-3 times a day to help soothe and heal a stye.
preparations.preparation_study: N/A
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Aloe barbadensis teas can help alleviate the symptoms of stye, but consuming it may cause side effects such as stomach upset, diarrhea, and allergic reactions in some individuals. To use Aloe barbadensis teas safely, it is recommended to start with a small dose and gradually increase the amount, avoid taking it for extended periods, and be cautious when combining it with other medications or substances that may interact with its active compounds.
preparation_usage: Wash hands before touching the stye
Soak a clean cloth in cooled Aloe barbadensis tea
Apply the soaked cloth to the stye for 10-15 minutes, 3 times a day
Repeat the application for 3-5 days to see improvement
Consult a doctor if the stye persists or worsens

preparations.herb_desc: Hypericum perforatum teas, also known as St. John's Wort tea, have been traditionally used to treat various ailments, including the stye. The anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties of this herbal preparation help to reduce swelling and prevent infection of the stye. The bioactive constituents, such as flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, in Hypericum perforatum tea possess antibacterial and antiviral activities that aid in treating the stye. By consuming this herbal preparation, individuals may benefit from its ability to reduce pain, inflammation, and the risk of infection associated with the stye.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of dried Hypericum perforatum flowers and 1 cup of boiling water.
Steep the dried flowers in boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the mixture into a cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth.
Add 1 tablespoon of honey (optional) to the tea and stir well.
Drink 1 cup of the tea 3 times a day for 3-5 days to help alleviate stye symptoms.
preparations.preparation_study: According to BMC veterinary research, Hypericum perforatum teas may be beneficial for stye treatment due to its antibacterial and antifungal effects, which can help combat the underlying bacterial or fungal infections that cause the condition.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Hypericum perforatum teas can be used to treat stye, but possible side effects include mild allergic reactions, such as skin irritation, and interactions with certain medications, like blood thinners and diabetes medications. To minimize potential side effects, it's essential to start with small doses, monitor your body's response, and avoid using Hypericum perforatum teas with other herbal remedies or medications, especially those that affect blood sugar or blood clotting.
preparation_usage: Soak a clean cloth in 1 cup of hot Hypericum perforatum tea.
Cool the tea to comfortable temperature for application.
Gently apply the cooled cloth to the affected eye area.
Leave the cloth on for 10-15 minutes, 3-4 times a day.
Continue for 3-5 days, or until the stye disappears.

preparations.herb_desc: Achillea millefolium teas, also known as yarrow tea, have been traditionally used to treat stye, a painful and unsightly bacterial infection of the eyelid. The anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties of this herbal preparation help to reduce swelling and combat bacterial growth, thereby promoting the healing of the affected area. The bioactive constituents of yarrow tea, including flavonoids, phenolic acids, and sesquiterpenes, possess potent antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory activities that aid in the treatment of stye. Regular consumption of Achillea millefolium teas may also help to reduce the risk of recurrence and alleviate symptoms associated with stye, making it a beneficial herbal remedy for this common eye ailment.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Achillea millefolium leaves and flowers.
Steep 1 tablespoon of the leaves and flowers in 1 cup of boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth.
Add 1 tablespoon of honey to the tea, if desired, to make it sweeter.
Drink the tea 3-4 times a day to help reduce the swelling and pain of a stye.
preparations.preparation_study: N/A
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Achillea millefolium teas can be effective in treating stye, but some possible side effects include allergic reactions, such as skin rashes or itching, and interactions with other medications, particularly blood thinners. Precautions to take when using Achillea millefolium teas to treat stye include using them in moderation, as excessive consumption may lead to stomach upset, and avoiding use during pregnancy or breastfeeding, as the effects on the fetus or baby are not well understood.
preparation_usage: Soak a clean cloth in hot Achillea millefolium tea.
Apply the cloth to the affected eye for 5-7 minutes.
Repeat the application 2-3 times a day for 3-5 days.
Use 1 cup of tea for each application, made from 1 tablespoon of dried herb.
Continue treatment for as long as symptoms persist, then stop.

preparations.herb_desc: Silybum marianum teas have been traditionally used to treat the stye ailment due to their anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents present in these teas, such as flavonoids and silymarin, help to reduce inflammation and combat bacterial infections that cause the stye. By reducing swelling and promoting healing, Silybum marianum teas can help to alleviate symptoms and clear up the stye. The benefits of using these teas include reduced pain and discomfort, accelerated healing, and a lower risk of scarring or long-term complications from the stye.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of Silybum marianum flowers and leaves. Dry them completely.
Measure 1 tablespoon of dried Silybum marianum flowers and leaves. Place them in a tea infuser or a small piece of cheesecloth.
Heat 1 cup of water in a pot. Bring it to a boil, then reduce the heat.
Steep the Silybum marianum flowers and leaves in the hot water for 5-7 minutes. Strain the tea using the infuser or cheesecloth.
Drink the tea 2-3 times a day to help reduce the inflammation and speed up the healing process of a stye.
preparations.preparation_study: According to "Fitoterapia", Silybum marianum teas are used for treating sties, based on traditional folk medicine in Calabria, Southern Italy.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Silybum marianum teas can be used to treat the stye ailment, but it may cause side effects such as allergic reactions, stomach upset, and diarrhea in some individuals. When using this herbal preparation, it is recommended to take precautions such as starting with a small dose, monitoring your body's response, and avoiding excessive consumption to minimize potential side effects and ensure safe usage.
preparation_usage: Soak a cotton ball in the tea and apply to the stye.
Repeat application 3-4 times a day for 3-5 days.
Use 1-2 cups of Silybum marianum tea as a compress.
Steep 2-3 tablespoons of dried herb in 1 cup of boiling water.

Regular consumption of Taraxacum officinale teas may provide relief from the discomfort and pain associated with stye, as well as support overall eye health.

Chop the leaves and flowers finely and place them in a heat-resistant cup.
Pour 1 cup of boiling water over the chopped Taraxacum officinale.

The anti-inflammatory properties of this herbal preparation help to reduce swelling and ease pain associated with stye. The bioactive constituents of Urtica dioica, including flavonoids, phenolic acids, and lignans, possess antimicrobial and antioxidant activities that help to combat bacterial and viral infections that cause stye. By consuming Urtica dioica teas, individuals can benefit from its ability to reduce inflammation, promote healing, and prevent future stye occurrences, making it a popular natural remedy for this common eye ailment.

Steep 1 tablespoon of the leaves and flowers in 1 cup of boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the mixture into a cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth.
Add 1 tablespoon of honey to the tea, if desired, and mix well.
Drink the tea 2-3 times a day to help reduce inflammation and promote healing for stye.
preparations.preparation_study: According to "Molecules (Basel, Switzerland)", Urtica dioica teas for stye may not be directly mentioned in the study, but the plant's extract has shown potential in wound healing, including increasing cell proliferation and migration, anti-inflammatory properties, and antioxidant properties, which may be beneficial for treating stye due to its ability to promote faster wound healing.

preparations.herb_desc: Aloe vera teas have been used as a natural remedy to treat the stye ailment, a bacterial infection causing painful swelling on the eyelid. The anti-inflammatory properties of aloe vera teas help to reduce redness and swelling, while its antibacterial properties combat the underlying infection. The bioactive constituents present in aloe vera teas, such as aloin and aloe-emodin, possess antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory activities that aid in treating the stye ailment. The benefits of using aloe vera teas to treat stye include reduced pain, inflammation, and risk of infection, making it a popular natural remedy for this common eye condition.
preparation_procedure: Cut 2-3 inches of Aloe vera leaf from a mature plant.
Scrape out the clear gel inside the leaf with a spoon.
Mix 1 tablespoon of Aloe vera gel with 1 cup of warm water.
Add 1 teaspoon of honey (optional) to the mixture for taste.
Drink the Aloe vera tea 2-3 times a day for up to 5 days to help soothe a stye.
preparations.preparation_study: According to the Journal of Education and Health Promotion, Aloe vera teas for stye may be an effective and safe alternative treatment option for oral health care, as it is one of the most frequently reported herbal compounds in studies.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Aloe vera teas can be beneficial in treating stye, but it may cause some side effects such as allergic reactions, digestive issues, and interact with other medications due to its potential anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. To use aloe vera teas safely, take precautions to avoid consuming excessive amounts, as it may cause stomach upset, and also be aware that it may not be suitable for people with sensitive stomachs or those taking certain medications.

preparations.herb_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra teas have been traditionally used to treat the stye ailment, which is characterized by a painful and swollen lump on the eyelid or eyebrow area. The anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties of this herbal preparation help to reduce the swelling and fight off the bacterial or viral infection causing the stye. The bioactive constituents present in Glycyrrhiza glabra teas, including glycyrrhizin, flavonoids, and saponins, contribute to its therapeutic effects by reducing inflammation, inhibiting bacterial growth, and promoting wound healing. The benefits of using Glycyrrhiza glabra teas to treat the stye ailment include rapid relief from pain and swelling, prevention of scarring, and promotion of a speedy recovery.
preparation_procedure: Gather 2 cups of water and 1 tablespoon of dried Glycyrrhiza glabra root.
Steep 2 teaspoons of dried Glycyrrhiza glabra root in the water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the mixture into a cup and discard the root.
Add 1 teaspoon of honey to the tea, if desired.
Drink the tea 2-3 times a day to help soothe the stye.
preparations.preparation_study: According to Journal of the Medical Association of Thailand = Chotmaihet thangphaet, Glycyrrhiza glabra teas may be effective for stye treatment as its ethanolic extract showed strong antimicrobial activity against S. pyogenes, a common cause of acute pharyngitis.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Glycyrrhiza glabra teas can be an effective herbal remedy for treating styes, but possible side effects may include increased blood pressure, potassium loss, and an increased risk of edema in some individuals. Precautions should be taken to avoid overusing these teas, as prolonged consumption can lead to long-term health issues such as kidney damage and an imbalance in electrolyte levels in the body.

preparations.herb_desc: Sambucus nigra teas have been traditionally used to treat stye, a painful and often unsightly bacterial infection of the eyelid.
